original: The Edict of Pistres of Charles the Bald orders defensive measures against the Vikings
Large fire in church of Saint Madeleine of Vézelay, France, kills a thousand pilgrims and seriously damages the church
Count Alfonso I of Portugal proclaimed King
Constantinople recaptured by Nicaean forces under Alexios Strategopoulos for Emperor Michael VIII, re-establishing Byzantine Empire
Jews are expelled from Breslau, Silesia
Spanish conquest of Tripoli by Pedro Navarro for Aragon crown; over 3,000 killed and more than 5,000 inhabitants enslaved
San Cristobal de la Habana forms in Cuba
About 300 heretics burn in Vrijdagmarkt, Ghent
Sebastián de Belalcázar on his search of El Dorado founds the City of Santiago de Cali
The City of Guayaquil is founded by the Spanish Conquistador Francisco de Orellana and given the name Muy Noble y Muy Leal Ciudad de Santiago de Guayaquil
Don Diego de Losada founds the city of Santiago de Leon de Caracas, modern-day Caracas, the capital city of Venezuela
Amsterdam bans 45 Roman Catholics

Admiral George Somers scuttles the "Sea Venture" in eastern Bermuda, the storm-damaged flagship of the London Company bound for Jamestown, Virginia. Survivors construct two new ships and continue on to resupply the embattled colony.
Important Royalist base Scarborough Castle surrenders to parliamentarians after one of the bloodiest sieges of the English Civil War
Nikita Minin becomes patriarch of Russian Orthodox Church
Austrian Emperor Leopold I expels 4,000 Jews from Vienna
Don Juan Domingo Zuniga y Fonseca becomes governor-general of Southern Netherlands
American-English official Elihu Yale becomes Governor of Fort St. George, Madras, the East India's Company's major trading port in India
Ignacio de Maya founds the Real Santiago de las Sabinas, now known as Sabinas Hidalgo, Nuevo León, México
The Three Years War begins along the Maine and Massachusetts border
North Carolina becomes a royal colony
Seven Years' War: the island battery at Fortress Louisbourg in Nova Scotia is silenced and all French warships are destroyed or taken
British capture Fort Niagara from the French (Seven Years' War)
Dutch patriots in exile found "Bataafs Legion"
The Brunswick Manifesto is issued to the population of Paris, promising vengeance if the French Royal Family is harmed
The first stone of the Pontcysyllte Aqueduct is laid
Battle of Niagara Falls (Lundy's Lane), the bloodiest battle of the War of 1812, ends in a stalemate between invading American army and Canadian and British forces
Rebellion led by José de la Riva-Agüero begins in Lima against the local rule of Bernardo de Monteagudo
Costa Rica annexes Guanacaste from Nicaragua
First railroad accident in US, Granite Railway, Quincy, Massachusetts, kills one
Ibrahim Pasha's army attacks Jewish settlers in Hebron, Palestine
The first commercial use of an electric telegraph is successfully demonstrated by William Cooke and Charles Wheatstone between Euston and Camden Town in London
First battle at Custozza: Austrians under Radetzky defeat Italian
Gold is discovered in the Rogue River in Oregon
Joaquin Murrieta, the famous Californian bandit known as "Robin Hood of El Dorado," is killed by California Rangers
First US intercollegiate billiard match (Harvard vs. Yale)
Crittenden Resolution is passed, stating that the war is to be fought to preserve the Union and uphold the Constitution, not to alter slavery
Skirmish at Fort Fillmore, New Mexico Territory: Rebels attack Union troops
Skirmish at Barbee's Crossroads, Virginia
25th US Postmaster General: Alexander W. Randall of Wisconsin takes office
US Congress forms the Wyoming Territory from parts of the Dakota, Utah, and Idaho territories
Carousel patented by Wilhelm Schneider, Davenport, Iowa
First US troops land and occupy Puerto Rico at Guanica Bay
Emily Hobhouse addresses public meetings in Britain about the concentration camps during the South African War
Castle on top of Telegraph Hill, San Francisco, closes
Korea becomes a protectorate of Japan
France's Louis Blériot makes first airplane flight across the English Channel
Comoros is proclaimed a French colony

A meeting in Johannesburg, called by the South African Native National Congress, now the African National Congress, is attended by a large number of people from South Africa, Botswana, Lesotho, and Swaziland
Carl Weilman strikes out six times in a 15-inning game
Pittsburgh Pirates future Baseball Hall of Fame outfielder Max Carey scores five runs without a hit, reaching first base on an error and four walks as the Bucs defeat the Philadelphia Phillies 12-2
Germany's Social Democratic Party declares "No German blood for Austrian tyrant" in response to German support of Austria-Hungary
Serbia meets the deadlines of Austria-Hungary's ultimatum and gives a conciliatory reply, though Serbia is mobilizing
Explosion at Lake Erie and Cleveland Waterworks
Sir Thomas White introduces the first income tax in Canada as a "temporary" measure, with the lowest bracket at 4% and the highest at 25%
Annette Adams is sworn in as the first female district attorney in the US, in California
Race riot in Chester, Pennsylvania, kills three black people and two white people
AT&T begins broadcasting on WBAY (NYC, later WEAF, WNBC, WRCA, and WFAN)
German mark devalued to 600,000 marks = $1
Philadelphia Athletics triple steal in first and fourth innings against Cleveland
Failed Nazi coup in Austria
115-acre Orchard Beach opens in the Bronx
Jewish artisans are not allowed in Germany
Revolutionary offensive of Ebro Spain (Hollander Piet)
Fifth and last Dutch government of Prime Minister Hendrikus Colijn forms
New York Yankees starting pitcher Atley Donald sets American League rookie record for consecutive wins; defeats St. Louis Browns 5-1 for his 12th consecutive victory

American John Sigmund begins what will be the longest solo swim of all time, swimming for 89 hours and 46 minutes from St. Louis to Caruthersville, Missouri, in the Mississippi River, covering 292 miles
41-year-old pitcher Lefty Grove wins his 300th and final MLB career game as the Boston Red Sox defeat the Cleveland Indians 10-6 at Fenway Park in Boston
German troops strike at Tsymlyanskaya
First warship named after an African American is launched: USS Harmon, a Buckley-class destroyer
Opposition group Zwaantje forms in Delfzijl
RAF bombs Fokker aircraft factory in Amsterdam
Japanese banzai attack on Guam
Operation Cobra: US forces begin major offensive in Normandy with air bombardment
World War II: Operation Spring is one of Canada's bloodiest days, with 1,444 casualties, including 441 killed in a single day
WWII: first jet fighter used in combat, German Luftwaffe's Messerschmitt 262
The US detonates an underwater atomic bomb at Bikini Atoll in the Marshall Islands (the fifth atomic explosion)
Puerto Rico becomes a self-governing US commonwealth (Constitution Day)
NYC transit fare rises from 10 cents to 15 cents, first use of subway tokens
46 die in a collision between the SS Andrea Doria and the MS Stockholm off the coast of Nantucket
Jordan attacks UN Palestine Force
Monarchy in Tunisia is abolished in favor of a republic
Peter Loader takes a cricket hat-trick for England against West Indies at Headingley
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
The African Regroupment Party (PRA) holds its first congress in Cotonou
SR-N1 hovercraft crosses the English Channel from Calais to Dover in just over 2 hours
Compagnie Industrielle et Forestière (Indufor) forms in Brussels
Belgian Senate accepts law on language regulations
US, Russia, and Britain sign nuclear test ban treaty
Race riot in Rochester, NY
The Beatles' album "A Hard Day's Night" goes to #1 and stays at #1 for 14 weeks
Construction begins on the San Francisco Muni Metro (Market Street Subway)
US health officials concede African Americans were used as guinea pigs in a 40-year syphilis experiment
USSR launches Mars 5

Marvin Hamlisch and Edward Kleban's musical "A Chorus Line" opens at the Shubert Theatre in NYC, setting a record for the longest-running Broadway show with 6,137 performances and winning 10 Tony Awards
Annegret Richter runs the 100 m in 11.01
Philip Glass and Robert Wilson's opera "Einstein on the Beach" premieres at the Théâtre Municipal in Avignon, France

The Cerro Maravilla Incident: Puerto Rico Police ambush and murder pro-independence activists Carlos Enrique Soto-Arriví (18) and Arnaldo Darío Rosado-Torres (24); initial investigation finds no official wrongdoing, but later evidence results in four police officers being convicted of murder and six others of perjury and obstruction of justice
109 cm of rainfall in Alvin, Texas (state record)
Train crash at Winsum, killing nine
US performs nuclear test at Nevada test site
Anti-apartheid protesters in Hamilton, New Zealand, force the cancellation of a rugby match between the provincial team Waikato and South Africa’s Springboks by invading the pitch during the game
France performs nuclear test
Zail Singh is sworn in as the seventh President of India
First non-human primate (baboon) conceived in a lab dish in San Antonio
Washington Public Power Supply System defaults on $2.25 billion
Cosmonaut Svetlana Savitskaya becomes the first woman to walk in space
English middle-distance runner Steve Cram runs a world record in the 1 mile (3:46.32) at Bislett Stadium in Oslo, Norway
Uganda suspends constitution following coup
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Sikh extremists kill 16 Hindus in Muktsar, India
R. Venkataraman becomes the eighth President of India
USSR launches Kosmos 1870, a 15-ton Earth observation satellite
Nadezhda Ryashkina of the USSR sets the women's 10,000 m walk record in 41:56.23
US Ambassador tells Iraq US won't take sides in Iraq-Kuwait dispute
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Howard Stern adds a fourth radio market, KLSX FM-97.1 in Los Angeles, California
Pittsburgh Steelers guard Terry Long is treated for an apparent suicide attempt after he learns he tested positive for steroid use
Seattle's Jay Buhner hits a 479-foot home run in Yankee Stadium
25th Olympic Games open in Barcelona, Spain
Army refuses to overturn 127-year-old conviction against Dr Mudd
Israeli offensive against terrorist bases in southern Lebanon
The St. James Church massacre in Kenilworth, Cape Town, South Africa, by the Azanian People's Liberation Army
Jordan and Israel end 46-year state of war (Washington, D.C.)
A gas bottle explodes in Saint-Michel station of line B of the RER (Paris regional train network), killing 8 and injuring 80
Kim LaPlante of Washington State is crowned Mrs. United States
Canadian rocker Rick Danko (The Band) gets suspended sentence in Japan for drug smuggling
Howard Stern is fired from radio station KEGL in Dallas, Texas
K. R. Narayanan is sworn in as India's 10th president and the first Dalit, formerly called "untouchable," to hold the office
Scientists announce the first human stem cells to be cultured in a laboratory using tissue taken from aborted human embryos
Vincent "The Chin" Gigante found guilty of racketeering in New York City
Air France Flight 4590, a Concorde supersonic passenger jet, F-BTSC, crashes just after takeoff from Paris, killing all 109 aboard and four on the ground
Faced with declining oil prices, OPEC ministers agree to cut crude oil production quotas by about 4%, or one million barrels per day
17th Commonwealth Games open in Manchester, England
Pratibha Patil is sworn in as India's first female President
Italy's credit rating is downgraded to CCC+ by Egan-Jones
Ivica Dačić is sworn in as Prime Minister of Serbia
The 2012 Summer Olympics begin (Opening ceremony 27th)
The double-dip recession in the UK economy continues with a fall of 0.7% in GDP in the second quarter of 2012
The UK economy returns to recession with a fall of 0.2% in GDP in the first quarter of 2012 following a fall of 0.3% in the last quarter of 2011
Israeli airstrike kills leader of Islamic Jihad's military wing, Salah Abu Hassanein
Palestinian officials call for a "Day of Rage" in the West Bank and within Israel against Israel's operation in Gaza; Israeli Defense Forces prepare for protests
UCI BMX Supercross series: Niek Kimmann and Stefany Hernandez win the world title
England fast bowler Jimmy Anderson becomes the first quick to take 50 wickets against all other seven major Test cricket-playing nations during the second Test against Pakistan in Manchester
Verizon announces $4.83 billion purchase of Yahoo
Israeli authorities remove new metal detectors from Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif after Palestinian protests amid rising tensions in Jerusalem
Constantine Iis proclaimed Roman Emperor by his troops
Foundation stone is laid for King's College Chapel in Cambridge by KingHenry VI, one of England's finest medieval buildings (main structure completed in 1515)
Portuguese forces underAfonso de Albuquerquefirst assault the prosperous trading city of Malacca, Malay Peninsula
Henry IIof France is crowned King of France
Maximilian IIsucceeds his father Ferdinand I as Holy Roman Emperor
Battle of Arnay-le-Duc, Burgundy: Huguenot victory forcesCharles IXof France to agree to a peace treaty that ends the Third War of Religion
France's Protestant KingHenry IVconverts to Roman Catholic faith
Horatio Nelsonloses more than 300 men and his right arm during the failed conquest attempt of Tenerife, Spain
French-Egyptian forces underNapoleonI beat Turks at Battle of Abukir
English engineerGeorge Stephensonintroduces his first steam locomotive, a traveling engine designed for hauling coal on the Killingworth wagonway, named Blücher
GeneralAgustín de Iturbideis crowned Agustín I, the first Emperor of Mexico
Charles Xsigns the July Ordinances, suspending freedom of the press, among other effects
Walter Huntreceives the first U.S. patent for a paper shirt collar
David Farragutis appointed as the first admiral in the US Navy
Ulysses S. Grantis named the first General of the Army
The ship Marco Polo, once the fastest clipper in the world, runs aground on Prince Edward Island. Spectators of the event includeLucy Maud Montgomery.
WriterJack Londonsails to join the Klondike Gold Rush, where he writes his first successful stories
Three years after winning the title from English boxerBob Fitzsimmons, American championJames J. Jeffriesrepeats the feat with an eighth-round knockout in San Francisco for the heavyweight crown
First Dutch live radio concert: American jazz orchestra led byDuke Ellington
US PresidentFranklin D. Rooseveltbans sale of benzine and gasoline to Japan
Dean MartinandJerry Lewisstage their first show as a comedy team at the 500 Club in Atlantic City, New Jersey
St. Louis CardinalStan Musialhits for the cycle, defeating Brooklyn 14-1
Pittsburgh Pirates'Roberto Clementehits MLB's first and only walk-off inside-the-park grand slam for a 9-8 win over visiting Chicago Cubs at Forbes Field
In a speech, US PresidentJohn F. Kennedyemphasizes that any attack on Berlin is an attack on NATO
New York Yankees outfielderRoger Marishits home runs 37, 38, 39, and 40 off four Chicago White Sox pitchers in a doubleheader sweep at Yankee Stadium, NYC
Bob Dylanis booed by sections of the crowd at the Newport Folk Festival for performing with an electric guitar, beginning the era of folk rock
Brian Jones' final performance as a Rolling Stone
The Supremesrelease single "You Can't Hurry Love"
PopePaul VIpublishes the encyclical "Humanae Vitae (Of Human Life)," which rejects any artificial forms of birth control

70,000 attend Seattle Pop Festival (across 3 days); performers includeBo Diddley, Flying Burrito Brothers, Ten Years After, Guess Who, Santana, Tim Buckley, Byrds, Ike &Tina TurnerRevue, Chicago Transit Authority,Chuck Berry, Led Zeppelin, and The Doors
Edward Kennedypleads guilty to leaving the scene of an accident a week after the Chappaquiddick car accident that killed Mary Jo Kopechne
Burt Bacharachand Hal David's song "(They Long to Be) Close to You," as sung by the Carpenters, reaches #1
George Harrisonpays £1,000,000 in tax on his Bangladesh benefit concert and album
RCA releasesDavid Bowie's single "Fame" in the UK, featuring a cameo appearance byJohn Lennon, and it peaks at no. 17
John Lydonforms rock group Public Image Ltd
Pete Roseof the Cincinnati Reds sets a National League record by hitting in 38 consecutive games
Robert Granville "Bob" Lemon replacesBilly Martinas Yankee manager

Atlantic Records and Albert Productions release "Back in Black," the seventh studio album byAC/DC, their first featuring lead singer Brian Johnson. It is a worldwide smash, selling over 50 million copies.
Megaforce Records releasesMetallica's debut album "Kill 'Em All"
Spokeswoman forRock Hudsonconfirms he has AIDS
KC RoyalGeorge Bretthits for the cycle
Roseanne Barrsings the National Anthem at Cincinnati Reds-San Diego Padres MLB game
Romantic comedy film "Sleepless in Seattle," starringTom HanksandMeg Ryanand co-written and directed byNora Ephron, is released
Autumn Jackson is found guilty of trying to extort $40 million fromBill Cosby
Carroll O'Connoris found not guilty of slandering Harry Perzigian
QBBrett Favrere-signs with Green Bay Packers for $50 million for 7 years
86th Tour de France:Lance Armstrongwins the first of seven consecutive Tour de France titles but is later disqualified for doping
Patrick Kanesigns a three-year contract with the Chicago Blackhawks
British TV series "Sherlock," created by Steven Moffat and Mark Gatiss and starringBenedict CumberbatchandMartin Freeman, debuts
Both Israel and Hamas review US Secretary of StateJohn Kerry's proposal for an immediate ceasefire and meetings in Cairo
Maryland issues currency depictingGeorge IIIthe trampling Magna Carta
Benito Mussoliniis dismissed as Italian Prime Minister and arrested on the authority of King Victor Emmanuel III
Thietmar, German chronicler and Bishop of Merseburg, born in Hildesheim, Saxony (d. 1018)
Casimir I the Restorer, Polish nobleman (Duke of Poland, 1040-58), born in Kraków, Poland (d. c. 1058)

Kiyomasa Kato, Japanese warlord, born in Owari Province, Japan (d. 1611)
